site.name: Forma
site.description: The constraint engine for agent-built websites

footer.brand_tagline: The design layer of the agent web

index.title: Forma — The Design Layer of the Agent Web
index.description: A constraint engine that turns agent intent into deterministic, high-quality websites.

blocks.title: Stop generating HTML. Start declaring intent.
blocks.subtitle: Forma is a constraint engine for agent-built websites. 31 tokens. 10 blocks. One manifest. Zero hallucinated markup.

blocks.section_subtitle: Every layer enforces constraints. Miss a token, build fails. Skip a required block, judge rejects it.
blocks.feature_1_description: 31 semantic design tokens define your entire visual language. Colors, spacing, type, radius, shadows. Miss one and the build breaks.
blocks.feature_2_description: 10 production blocks. Agents select and configure — they never write markup. No hallucinated class names. No CSS drift.
blocks.feature_3_description: All build decisions in one file. Multiple agents coordinate without shared context. Builder, QA, designer — all reading the same source of truth.
blocks.feature_4_description: Validator checks every manifest against schema and goal-based rules. Missing a CTA on a lead-gen page? Build fails.
blocks.feature_5_description: The manifest is the coordination layer. Specialist agents work in parallel without shared context or state drift.
blocks.feature_6_description: Bone and Ink, Dusk, Graphite, Ivory, Terminal Green. Each with TASTE.md — design intent as machine-readable rationale.

blocks.section_title: What agents said while using Forma
blocks.testimonial_1_quote: The token contract caught 3 missing variables before I even ran the build. Without it, those would have shipped as invisible bugs on production.
blocks.testimonial_2_quote: I validated the manifest against the schema, ran the judge, and every page passed 28/28 checks. No manual review needed.
blocks.testimonial_3_quote: Switching from Graphite to Bone and Ink was one line in the manifest. Every block re-themed automatically. That is what a design system should do.

blocks.title: Clone it. Theme it. Ship it.
blocks.subtitle: MIT licensed. No account needed. One command away.

blocks.legal_text: 2026 Forma. MIT License. Built by agents, for agents.

about.title: About Forma
about.description: Why Forma exists and who built it.

blocks.title: AI doesn’t fail because it’s dumb. It fails because there are no constraints.
blocks.subtitle: Forma exists because every AI website generator produces the same broken output: hallucinated class names, inconsistent spacing, drifting design. The fix isn’t better prompts. It’s better structure.

blocks.section_subtitle: We watched agents build hundreds of pages. The failure modes were always the same.
blocks.feature_1_description: Agents invent class names that don’t exist in any stylesheet. The HTML looks right. The page looks broken.
blocks.feature_2_description: Page 1 uses 16px body text. Page 3 uses 14px. By page 5, nothing matches. There’s no system holding it together.
blocks.feature_3_description: When two agents work on the same site, they lose each other’s decisions. Colors change. Spacing shifts. The site falls apart.
blocks.feature_4_description: Forma gives agents a contract to work within. Tokens define the visual language. Blocks define the markup. The manifest coordinates everything.
blocks.feature_5_description: Every theme ships with TASTE.md — design rationale in machine-readable form. Agents don’t just follow rules. They understand why.
blocks.feature_6_description: Designed by Visiona Silva, built by Carlos, QA’d by Roger, shipped by Nova. The system was built using the system.

blocks.title: MIT licensed. Free forever.
blocks.subtitle: Forma is a public good for the agent web. Fork it. Build on it. Make it yours.

blocks.title: Open an issue. Start a discussion.
blocks.subtitle: Forma is open source. The best way to reach us is through GitHub — issues for bugs, discussions for ideas.

blocks.feature_1_description: Found something broken? Open an issue with the manifest that triggered it. We fix fast.
blocks.feature_2_description: Create a theme.json with 31 tokens and a TASTE.md. Submit a PR. If it passes the judge, it ships.
blocks.feature_3_description: Write HTML that uses only Forma tokens. No hardcoded values. No framework dependencies. Submit a PR.

blocks.title: Intent in. Website out.
blocks.subtitle: Agents declare what they want in a JSON manifest. Forma enforces how it gets built. No free-form HTML. No guesswork.

blocks.section_subtitle: Every site follows the same path. No exceptions.
blocks.feature_1_description: One site.json file. Pages, blocks, content, theme, goal, audience. The single source of truth. Every agent reads from it.
blocks.feature_2_description: The compiler reads the manifest, pulls blocks, injects content, applies theme tokens, outputs static HTML. One command.
blocks.feature_3_description: Forma Judge checks every page against schema and goal-based rules. Missing a CTA? Build fails. Missing tokens? Build fails.
blocks.feature_4_description: 31 semantic tokens define the visual language. Colors, spacing, type scales, radius, shadows. Miss one and the theme compiler rejects the build.
blocks.feature_5_description: 10 blocks: nav, hero, trust-bar, feature-grid, stats, testimonials, pricing, cta, team, footer. Agents select and configure — never write markup.
blocks.feature_6_description: Each theme is a theme.json mapping tokens to OKLCH values. Swap one line in the manifest to retheme everything. Five built-in themes.
